About

Interdisciplinary
Systems
Engineering

MSc Aerospace Engineer working across the full stack of physical systems design — from underwater vehicle hydrodynamics and UAV control architecture to LLM-assisted autonomy and consumer software at scale.

Currently building miniROV, an open-source tethered ROV with a locally-hosted LLM pipeline, with Phase 0 SITL validation complete. Previously designed and launched Kyupid, a Flutter app now at 150,000+ downloads worldwide.

Projects

Work

01 — Open Source

miniROV

Active Development

Tethered underwater ROV with LLM-assisted supervised autonomy for UK coastal and lake deployments. Natural language commands → Ollama (qwen2.5:14b) → structured JSON → pymavlink → ArduSub. Phase 0 complete: full pipeline validated against SITL. Manual override retained at all times.

ArduSub MAVLink / pymavlink Ollama · qwen2.5:14b Raspberry Pi 5 Navigator FC 4× T200 Thrusters Python SITL

System Architecture

Two-node topology over a 50 m tether. Topside: Apple M4 Pro running Ollama locally and a Python minirov-brain agent for command arbitration and safety envelopes. Subsea: Raspberry Pi 5 running ArduSub via Blue Robotics Navigator Hat, driving 4× T200 thrusters. Zero cloud dependency in the inference loop.

NL → MAVLink Pipeline

01
Natural Language Input
Plain-English operator commands — e.g. "descend to 3 m, hold north."
02
Ollama Inference (qwen2.5:14b)
Local LLM parses intent, returns structured JSON action payload. No external API.
03
minirov-brain Arbitration
Validates JSON schema, enforces safety envelopes, converts to MAVLink RC override via pymavlink.
04
ArduSub Execution
RC overrides reach ArduSub Stable-4.1 — stabilisation, depth hold, thruster mixing. Manual override always available.

Specifications

Topside ComputeApple M4 Pro (Ollama local)
Subsea ComputeRaspberry Pi 5
Flight ControllerBlue Robotics Navigator Hat
FirmwareArduSub Stable-4.1
Thrusters4× Blue Robotics T200
Tether50 m (comms + power)
LLMqwen2.5:14b via Ollama
Interfacepymavlink RC override
SimulationArduSub SITL (Ph.0 ✓)
TargetUK coastal & lake

Development Phases

Phase 0 — Software foundations & SITL ✓
Phase 1 — Electronics bench build
Phase 2 — Enclosure & frame assembly
Phase 3 — Pool and tank testing
Phase 4 — UK coastal field deployment
View on GitHub
02 — MSc Thesis

Underwater
Glider

Complete

Fully deployable buoyancy-driven glider platform fitted with a NaI nuclear scintillator for oceanic radiation mapping. Designed end-to-end in OnShape — watertight acrylic enclosure, NEMA-17 leadscrew buoyancy engine, centre-of-mass shifter — fabricated, assembled, and bath-tested.

Buoyancy Engine Design NaI Scintillator Hydrodynamics OnShape CAD SLA / FDM 3D Print Arduino + Motor Control Radiation Mapping

Full assembly — bench integration

Buoyancy engine prototype

Bath test — buoyancy validation

Design Rationale

Gliders offer far greater endurance-to-cost than propeller-driven AUVs. Gamma rays attenuate rapidly in seawater — the highest contamination concentrations occur in the upper few metres of the water column, making a shallow-survey glider the right platform for radiation mapping. Design minimised acoustic signature to protect scintillator SNR.

Buoyancy Engine

A NEMA-17 stepper + T8 stainless leadscrew drives a BD Plastipak syringe plunger, modulating displaced volume to control buoyancy. SLA-printed support plates constrain the syringe body on 6 mm carbon fibre rails. Pitch is trimmed by a second leadscrew mechanism translating the battery pack — no external control surfaces.

Sensing Payload

NaI scintillator for in-situ gamma detection, feeding a NanoPi onboard computer. Measurements geo-referenced against GPS fix and pressure-derived depth — producing depth-resolved radiation maps directly replicating post-Fukushima long-range survey methodology.

System Breakdown

EnclosureCast acrylic tube, 4″ (Ø114.3 mm)
End capsAluminium 6061-T6, Type III anodised
Buoyancy actuatorNEMA-17 + T8 SS leadscrew + LM6UU
SyringeBD Plastipak (modified plunger)
Rails6 mm carbon fibre
CoM shifterNEMA-17 leadscrew + battery tram
HydrofoilsLaser-cut acrylic, Al+SLA mounts
ElectronicsArduino Uno + motor controller
Power12V LiPo 550 mAh
PayloadNaI scintillator + NanoPi
CADOnShape — full assembly drawings

Lessons & Next Steps

Flat-plate wings to be replaced with symmetrical thin hydrofoil planform. Syringe/stepper buoyancy engine to be replaced with a compact higher-force actuator. Pressure sensor + IMU required to close the depth/pitch feedback loop. Roll control mechanism to be added.

03 — Team of Five

Tricopter
UAV

Complete

Custom tricopter UAV with servo-actuated thrust vectoring yaw — eliminating the counter-rotor — and an in-house electromagnetic pickup/release mechanism for 150 g vaccine payload delivery at 15-minute loaded endurance.

Thrust Vector Yaw Servo Actuation Carbon Fibre Frame 15 min Endurance 150 g Payload Electromagnet Release

Bench build — electronics integration

Servo vectoring mount — 3D printed

Indoor flight cage — first hover

Yaw Control Architecture

The aft rotor is mounted on a servo-driven vectoring mount, rotating ±30° to generate yaw torque — no counter-rotating propeller pair required. Tuned to minimise cross-coupling with roll/pitch. Avoids the weight and drag penalty of a fourth motor while retaining payload-fraction efficiency.

Payload Delivery Mechanism

In-house latched electromagnet release activated via flight controller auxiliary channel. Sized for a standard vaccine cold-chain pack (150 g max). Designed for field reliability under continuous vibration loading from three rotors.

Design Challenges

Vectoring mount introduces PID tuning complexity — aft rotor thrust vector rotation affects pitch authority at servo travel extremes, requiring mechanical travel limits and gain scheduling to prevent attitude excursions under payload.

Specifications

ConfigurationTricopter — 2 fixed + 1 vectored aft
Yaw mechanismServo-actuated thrust vector
FrameCarbon fibre arms
Max payload150 g (vaccine delivery)
Endurance15 min (loaded)
ReleaseLatched EM — FC AUX output
Team size5 engineers
04 — Live Product

Kyupid

Live · 150K+ Downloads

Productivity app for couples — 150,000+ downloads worldwide. Solo ownership end-to-end: product definition, UX architecture, and cross-platform engineering in Flutter for iOS and Android. Reactive architecture with real-time shared state between geographically separate users.

Flutter · Dart iOS & Android Real-time Sync Full-Stack UX Design 150K+ Downloads

Scope

Solo ownership across the full product lifecycle: market research → product definition → UX design → engineering → App Store / Play Store → growth. No external agency or co-founder. Engineering systems thinking applied directly to consumer software product development at meaningful scale.

Architecture

Flutter for a single codebase across iOS and Android with native rendering performance. Reactive state management handles real-time shared state between two users on separate devices with low perceived latency. Backend handles auth, data sync, and push notifications.
